name: Housing Counseling Services, Incorporated (Hsc,Inc.)
description_short: Non-profit housing counselor serving DC, Maryland, and Virginia since 1972. HCS provides free counseling for homebuyers, homeowners, renters, and those facing homelessness.
description_long:
Housing Counseling Services, Incorporated (HCS) is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization established in 1972 to address housing challenges for low- and moderate-income residents across the Washington, DC metropolitan region, including Maryland and Virginia. The organization operates from its headquarters at 2410 17th Street NW in Washington, DC, and has built nearly five decades of experience serving vulnerable populations in the housing market.

HCS operates a comprehensive "one-stop-shop" model providing housing counseling, training, advocacy, and technical assistance across six primary service areas: homelessness prevention, homebuyer preparation, homeowner support, tenant rights and eviction prevention, HIV/AIDS housing assistance, and veteran housing stability. For homebuyers, they offer credit review, debt and savings assessment, and access to home purchase assistance programs. For existing homeowners, they provide foreclosure prevention counseling, home repair guidance, reverse mortgage education, and refinancing support. Tenants receive eviction prevention assistance, landlord-tenant dispute resolution, and credit preparation services. The organization also administers specialized programs including the Support Services for Veteran Families (SSVF) program and the MHAP Program for persons living with HIV/AIDS.

HCS distinguishes itself through its targeted focus on underserved populations and comprehensive service integration rather than single-issue counseling. The organization provides specialized case management services, including temporary financial assistance for veterans and extensive referral databases for persons living with HIV/AIDS. Their dual approach of direct counseling combined with advocacy and technical assistance to housing organizations demonstrates institutional commitment beyond individual consumer advising.

As a legitimate non-profit with nearly 50 years of operation, HCS offers genuinely free services without hidden fees or for-profit motives. However, the organization's website indicates ongoing construction, and specific service availability may vary by location within the DC, Maryland, and Virginia region. Consumers should contact them directly to confirm service eligibility and current program availability.
pros: Established 501(c)(3) non-profit with 50+ years of operation (founded 1972)
Comprehensive services including foreclosure prevention, eviction prevention, and homelessness resolution
Specialized programs for underserved populations: veterans, persons living with HIV/AIDS, and homeless individuals
Free counseling and case management services with no profit motive
Multi-jurisdictional coverage across DC, Maryland, and Virginia metropolitan region
Provides temporary financial assistance for eligible veterans through SSVF program
Offers both individual counseling and technical assistance to housing organizations
cons: Website is under construction, limiting accessibility to detailed program information
Email contact information is obscured/redacted on website, making direct contact difficult
Services limited to DC, Maryland, and Virginia region only
No online intake or application process visible; requires phone call or in-person visit
Limited information about response times, wait periods, or service availability windows
best_for: Low- and moderate-income first-time homebuyers preparing for home purchase
Homeowners facing foreclosure or seeking refinancing/reverse mortgage counseling
Renters at risk of eviction or displacement
diagnosis: HCS is best for low- to moderate-income residents in the DC metropolitan area seeking free, non-profit housing counseling without sales pressure or upsells. Primary caveat: geographical limitation to DC, Maryland, and Virginia, and the organization's website construction status may impact information accessibility and service responsiveness.
services: Homelessness prevention and case management services
Credit review and debt assessment for homebuyers
Home purchase assistance program access and navigation
Eviction prevention and landlord-tenant dispute resolution
Rent preparation and credit/debt assessment for tenants
Housing assistance referrals for persons living with HIV/AIDS (MHAP Program)
Support Services for Veteran Families (SSVF) case management
Temporary financial assistance for eligible veterans
Technical assistance to cooperative and condominium associations
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
